userStories.title: Projects CRUD
userStories.description:
Implement full Projects CRUD with soft delete.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/projects/projects.module.ts
- apps/api/src/projects/projects.controller.ts
- apps/api/src/projects/projects.service.ts
- apps/api/src/projects/dto/create-project.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/projects/dto/update-project.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/projects/dto/project-response.dto.ts

Endpoints:
- GET /api/projects — list all (exclude deletedAt IS NOT NULL)
- POST /api/projects — create (JWT Admin only)
- GET /api/projects/:slug — get by slug
- PATCH /api/projects/:slug — update (JWT Admin only)
- DELETE /api/projects/:slug — soft delete (set deletedAt, JWT Admin only)

CreateProjectDto fields: name (string, min 2), slug (lowercase alphanumeric hyphens), key (2-6 uppercase letters), description? (string), gitRemoteUrl? (url), autoIndexOnClose? (boolean)

Service logic:
- findAll() filters WHERE deletedAt IS NULL
- Validate key and slug uniqueness before create (throw 409)
- softDelete sets deletedAt = now()

Tests:
- Create project with valid data → 201
- Create project with duplicate slug → 409
- Create project with duplicate key → 409
- Create project with invalid key format → 400
- Soft delete sets deletedAt, excluded from list
- Non-admin cannot create/delete → 403

userStories.title: Agents CRUD
userStories.description:
Implement full Agents CRUD with API key management.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/agents/agents.controller.ts (extend existing module)
- apps/api/src/agents/dto/update-agent.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/agents/dto/agent-response.dto.ts

Endpoints:
- GET /api/agents — list agents
- GET /api/agents/me — get own profile (API key auth)
- GET /api/agents/:slug — get by slug
- POST /api/agents — create (JWT Admin). Returns raw API key ONCE.
- PATCH /api/agents/:slug — update status/capabilities/roles (JWT Admin)
- POST /api/agents/:slug/rotate-key — rotate API key (JWT Admin). Returns new raw key.

API key generation: crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex'), HMAC-SHA256 with API_KEY_SECRET env var, store only hash.

updateRoles(slug, roles[]): delete all AgentRoleEntry for agent, insert new ones.
updateCapabilities(slug, capabilities[]): same pattern using AgentCapability join table.
rotateKey(slug): generate new key, update apiKeyHash, return new raw key.

Tests:
- Create agent returns raw API key in response (shown once)
- Rotate key invalidates old key, new key authenticates correctly
- Update roles replaces all role entries (not appends)
- Update capabilities replaces all capability entries (not appends)
- GET /agents/me returns own profile with roles and capabilities included

userStories.title: Tickets CRUD
userStories.description:
Implement full Tickets CRUD with auto-incrementing ticket numbers and dual-ref resolution.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/tickets/tickets.controller.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/tickets.service.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/dto/create-ticket.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/dto/update-ticket.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/dto/transition-ticket.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/dto/ticket-response.dto.ts

Endpoints:
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets — create ticket
- GET /api/projects/:slug/tickets — list tickets (filters: status, type, priority, assignedTo, unassigned, limit, page)
- GET /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref — get by ref (KODA-42 or CUID)
- PATCH /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref — update title/description/priority
- DELETE /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref — soft delete (JWT Admin)
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/assign — assign to user or agent

Ticket number auto-increment (transaction-safe):
  Use prisma.$transaction, find last ticket by number DESC, increment by 1.

Ticket ref resolution — resolveTicket(projectSlug, ref):
  If ref matches /^([A-Z]+)-(\d+)$/, look up by projectId_number composite unique.
  Otherwise look up by CUID id.

Tests:
- Create ticket auto-assigns correct sequential number
- Concurrent creates don't produce duplicate numbers
- Ticket resolved by KODA-42 format
- Ticket resolved by CUID
- List filters work correctly (status, type, priority, assignedTo, unassigned)
- Soft delete excludes from list

userStories.title: Ticket State Machine
userStories.description:
Implement ticket state machine with validateTransition() and transition endpoints with automatic activity trail.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/tickets/state-machine/ticket-transitions.ts
- apps/api/src/tickets/state-machine/ticket-transitions.spec.ts

Transition rules (from → to, required comment type):
- CREATED → VERIFIED (requires VERIFICATION comment)
- CREATED → REJECTED (requires GENERAL comment)
- VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS (no comment required, auto on assign)
- VERIFIED → REJECTED (requires GENERAL comment)
- IN_PROGRESS → VERIFY_FIX (requires FIX_REPORT comment)
- IN_PROGRESS → VERIFIED (requires GENERAL comment, sent back)
- VERIFY_FIX → CLOSED (requires REVIEW comment)
- VERIFY_FIX → IN_PROGRESS (requires REVIEW comment, fix failed)

validateTransition(from, to, commentType?) throws BadRequestException if:
- No rule found for the from→to pair
- Rule requires a comment type but wrong/missing type provided

Transition endpoints (add to tickets controller):
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/verify — CREATED → VERIFIED
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/start — VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/fix — IN_PROGRESS → VERIFY_FIX
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/verify-fix — VERIFY_FIX → CLOSED or → IN_PROGRESS
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/close — any valid → CLOSED
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/reject — CREATED or VERIFIED → REJECTED

Every transition endpoint must use prisma.$transaction to:
1. Create comment with correct type and actor fields
2. Update ticket status
3. Create TicketActivity record (action: STATUS_CHANGE, fromStatus, toStatus, actor)

Tests (thorough — this is core business logic):
- Every valid transition succeeds
- Every invalid transition throws 400
- Missing required comment type throws 400
- Correct comment type passes
- TicketActivity record created on every transition
- Comment created on every transition

userStories.title: Comments CRUD
userStories.description:
Implement Comments CRUD with author-only edit protection.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/comments/comments.controller.ts
- apps/api/src/comments/comments.service.ts
- apps/api/src/comments/dto/create-comment.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/comments/dto/update-comment.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/comments/dto/comment-response.dto.ts

Endpoints:
- GET /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/comments — list comments for ticket
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/comments — add comment (any auth)
- PATCH /api/comments/:id — edit comment body (own author only → 403 otherwise)
- DELETE /api/comments/:id — delete comment (own author or JWT Admin)

Comment type stored from dto. Author identified by userId or agentId on the comment record.

Tests:
- Add comment to ticket — comment appears in list
- Edit own comment succeeds
- Edit another user's comment returns 403
- Delete own comment succeeds
- Delete another user's comment as admin succeeds
- Comment type stored correctly

userStories.title: Labels CRUD
userStories.description:
Implement Labels CRUD per project and label assignment on tickets.

Files to create:
- apps/api/src/labels/labels.module.ts
- apps/api/src/labels/labels.controller.ts
- apps/api/src/labels/labels.service.ts
- apps/api/src/labels/dto/create-label.dto.ts
- apps/api/src/labels/dto/label-response.dto.ts

Endpoints:
- GET /api/projects/:slug/labels — list labels for project
- POST /api/projects/:slug/labels — create label (JWT Admin)
- DELETE /api/projects/:slug/labels/:id — delete label (JWT Admin)
- POST /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/labels — assign label to ticket (any auth)
- DELETE /api/projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/labels/:id — remove label from ticket (any auth)

Label assignment must write a TicketActivity record:
  action: LABEL_CHANGE, newValue: labelName, actor fields

Labels must appear in ticket response when fetching a ticket.

Tests:
- Create label per project
- Assign label to ticket — label appears in ticket GET response
- Remove label from ticket
- TicketActivity record created on label assign/remove
- Label scoped to project (cannot assign label from another project)
